  • API Reference
  • Arrays
  • Arrays


Arrays

KWaveArray represents a multi-element transducer array with elements specified in physical coordinates (metres). Use it when you need distributed source/sensor signals across multiple geometric elements.

Element Types

KWave.ArrayElement — Type
ArrayElement

Abstract supertype for all transducer array element geometries. Concrete subtypes: ArcElement, BowlElement, DiscElement, RectElement, SphereElement.

source
KWave.ArcElement — Type

Arc element in 2D.

source
KWave.BowlElement — Type

Bowl element in 3D.

source
KWave.DiscElement — Type

Disc element in 3D (flat circular piston).

source
KWave.RectElement — Type

Rectangular element in 3D.

source
KWave.SphereElement — Type

Sphere segment element in 3D.

source

Array

KWave.KWaveArray — Type
KWaveArray

Off-grid transducer array model for multi-element transducers.

Elements are defined in physical coordinates (not grid points) and mapped to the computational grid via get_element_binary_mask.

source
KWave.add_arc_element! — Function
add_arc_element!(array, position, radius, diameter, focus)

Add a 2D arc element to the array.

Arguments

  • position: (x, y) center of curvature [m]
  • radius: Radius of curvature [m]
  • diameter: Aperture diameter [m]
  • focus: (fx, fy) focus position [m]
source
KWave.add_bowl_element! — Function
add_bowl_element!(array, position, radius, diameter, focus)

Add a 3D bowl element to the array.

source
KWave.add_disc_element! — Function
add_disc_element!(array, position, diameter, focus)

Add a 3D flat disc element to the array.

source
KWave.add_rect_element! — Function
add_rect_element!(array, position, width, height, focus)

Add a 3D rectangular element to the array.

source

Grid Projection & Signal Distribution

KWave.get_element_binary_mask — Function
get_element_binary_mask(array, kgrid, element_index)

Get the binary mask for a single element mapped to the computational grid.

Arguments

  • array: KWaveArray
  • kgrid: KWaveGrid
  • element_index: Index of element to map

Returns

Binary mask (BitArray) of grid size.

source
KWave.get_array_binary_mask — Function
get_array_binary_mask(array, kgrid)

Get the combined binary mask for all elements in the array.

Returns

Binary mask (BitArray) of grid size with true at all element locations.

source
KWave.get_distributed_source_signal — Function
get_distributed_source_signal(array, kgrid, source_signal)

Distribute a per-element source signal across grid points for each element.

For elements that map to multiple grid points, the source signal is divided equally among those points.

Arguments

  • array: KWaveArray
  • kgrid: KWaveGrid
  • source_signal: Matrix of size (num_elements × Nt) source signals

Returns

(combined_mask, distributed_signal) — combined binary mask and matrix of grid-point signals (numgridpoints × Nt).

source
KWave.combine_sensor_data — Function
combine_sensor_data(array, kgrid, sensor_data)

Combine grid-point sensor data back into per-element data by averaging over each element's grid points.

Arguments

  • array: KWaveArray
  • kgrid: KWaveGrid
  • sensor_data: Matrix of recorded sensor data (numsensorpoints × Nt)

Returns

Matrix of per-element sensor data (num_elements × Nt).

source

Typical Workflow

# Build a linear array of arc elements
arr = KWaveArray()
for i in 1:8
    add_arc_element!(arr; position=[i*1e-3, 0.0], radius=5e-3, diameter=2e-3, focus=[i*1e-3, 5e-3])
end

# Project onto grid
mask = get_array_binary_mask(arr, kgrid)    # BitMatrix (Nx × Ny)

# Distribute a per-element source signal
source_signal = tone_burst(10e6, 5e6, 3)   # single element signal
dist_signal = get_distributed_source_signal(arr, kgrid, source_signal)
# dist_signal: Matrix (n_source_points × Nt)

# Set up source
source = KWaveSource(p_mask=mask, p=dist_signal)
« GeometryTransducer »
